content.problemStatement:
Today, you finally have a chance to compete with a famous coder like Chef and prove your mettle.

Chef has nnn distinct points in 2-D plane, i.e. there are never two points with both xxx and yyy coordinate equal and there are no two points with the same xxx coordinate. Consider a subset of the above nnn points which can be arranged as a sequence p1,…,pkp_1, \dots, p_kp1​,…,pk​, such that xp1<xp2<⋯<xpkx_{p_1} < x_{p_2} < \dots < x_{p_k}xp1​​<xp2​​<⋯<xpk​​ and each point pip_ipi​, where iii is in the range between 3 and kkk (both inclusive), lies below or on the line passing between points pi−1p_{i-1}pi−1​ and pi−2p_{i-2}pi−2​. Chef likes these kinds of subsets. So he wants to find the size of the largest of such subsets.


Input Format
First line of the input contains one integer TTT - number of tests. TTT tests follow.


First line of each test contains one integer NNN - number of points.


The next NNN lines of each test case contain integer point coordinates xix_ixi​, yiy_iyi​.



Output Format
For each test case, output a single integer in a separate line corresponding to the largest subset size.
constraints: 1≤T,N1 \leq T, N1≤T,N, sum over all NNN over all test cases ≤2000\leq 2000≤2000

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Explanation case 1: We can get the sequence (1, 1) - (4, 2) - (9, 3), where point (9, 3) lies below the line between points (1, 1) and (4, 2).
Explanation case 2: We can get the sequence (1, 1) - (4, 2) - (9, 3).
Explanation case 3: We can get the sequence (1, 2) - (2, 3) - (3, 2) - (4, 1).
